skill.name: example-skill
skill.description: A template skill that shows the standard sections for writing AI skill instructions.
skill.summary: Use this template to draft structured skill documentation for AI coding assistants.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only placeholder prose and markdown code fences. No external commands, executable scripts, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections are markdown code block delimiters in template documentation. They do not execute commands or instruct the assistant to run shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is a markdown fence around example text. There is no command string, interpreter call, or executable instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections are substring matches in placeholder prose. No cryptographic function, hash call, encryption library, or credential use appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are frontmatter and overview placeholder text. Semantic review found no crypto operation or security-sensitive data flow.

content.user_title: Create Skill Documentation From a Template
content.value_statement: Skill authors need a clear starting point for consistent AI instructions. This template provides standard sections for overview, protocol, examples, red flags, requirements, and related links.
actual_capabilities: Provides a named SKILL.md template with frontmatter.
Defines sections for overview, activation situations, and protocol steps.
Includes good and bad example placeholders for skill authors.
Lists red flag prompts that guide protocol adherence.
Reserves space for requirements and related documentation links.
limitations: The file is a placeholder and does not implement domain-specific behavior.
Examples must be replaced before real users can rely on the skill.
It does not include scripts, tests, assets, or automation.
It provides structure only, not validation of completed skill content.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Skill
use_cases.description: Start from a consistent structure before adding domain instructions, examples, requirements, and related resources.

use_cases.title: Standardize Documentation
use_cases.description: Use the same headings and protocol format across multiple internal assistant skills.

use_cases.title: Review Skill Completeness
use_cases.description: Compare a draft skill against template sections to identify missing guidance or examples.

prompt_templates.title: Fill the Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this template to write an overview for a skill that helps with release notes.

prompt_templates.title: Define Activation Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three clear situations where this skill should activate for database migration reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Write Protocol Steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this skill idea into a numbered protocol with concise details for each step.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Completed Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my completed skill draft against this template and identify gaps, unclear triggers, and weak examples.

output_examples.input: Create activation situations for a skill that reviews pull requests.
output: Use when a user asks for a pull request review.
Use when a user requests risk analysis for changed code.
Use when a user asks whether tests cover a proposed change.

output_examples.input: Improve the protocol section for a documentation skill.
output_examples.output: A revised protocol with ordered steps for reading context, identifying missing information, drafting updates, and checking terminology consistency.

output_examples.input: Find missing sections in my draft skill.
output_examples.output: A completeness review that notes missing examples, undefined requirements, and absent related documentation links.

best_practices: Replace every placeholder before publishing the skill.
Keep activation situations specific and easy to verify.
Include one realistic good example and one realistic bad example.
anti_patterns: Publishing the template without replacing placeholder text.
Using vague triggers that activate the skill too broadly.
Adding instructions that override system or developer policies.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It provides a documentation template for creating structured AI skill instructions.

faq.question: Is this a finished user workflow?
faq.answer: No. It is a starter template that needs domain-specific content before publication.

faq.question: Does it run code or commands?
faq.answer: No. The file contains markdown documentation only.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should authors customize first?
faq.answer: Authors should replace the description, activation situations, protocol steps, examples, and requirements.

faq.question: Can marketplace reviewers use it?
faq.answer: Yes. Reviewers can compare draft skills against the template sections for completeness.
